description: Sequential API calls multiply load time and block rendering
do: Parallelize independent requests with Promise.all or concurrent queries
dont: Don't chain fetch calls sequentially when data is independent

description: Fetching independent data in parallel cuts load time significantly
do: Use Promise.all or React Query parallel queries for independent data
dont: Don't await each request one by one when they have no dependencies

description: Suspense boundaries control loading states at component boundaries
do: Wrap data-fetching components with Suspense and meaningful fallbacks
dont: Don't wrap entire app in a single Suspense boundary — use granular placement

description: Data fetching libraries handle caching deduplication and revalidation automatically
do: Use staleTime and cacheTime to control freshness and reduce refetches
dont: Don't set staleTime to 0 for data that rarely changes — causes excess requests

description: Optimistic updates make the UI feel instant by assuming success
do: Apply optimistic updates for mutations and roll back on error
dont: Don't wait for server confirmation before updating the UI for common actions

description: Unbounced search fires a request per keystroke overwhelming the server
do: Debounce search input by 300ms and cancel in-flight requests
dont: Don't fire API requests on every onChange event for search inputs

description: Stale responses can overwrite fresh data when requests resolve out of order
do: Use AbortController to cancel in-flight requests on new fetches or unmount
dont: Don't ignore cleanup — stale closures and responses cause subtle bugs

description: Unhandled errors crash the entire React tree without error boundaries
do: Place error boundaries around route segments and critical UI sections
dont: Don't let a single failing widget crash the entire application

description: Loading all code upfront delays initial render especially on mobile
do: Use React.lazy and dynamic import() for route-level code splitting
dont: Don't import heavy components statically if they're not needed immediately

description: Importing entire libraries when you need one function bloats the bundle
do: Use named imports and ensure dependencies support ESM tree shaking
dont: Don't import the entire lodash library — use lodash-es or individual imports

description: Barrel files (index.ts re-exports) defeat tree shaking and increase bundle size
do: Import directly from the source module path not through barrel files
dont: Don't create index.ts files that re-export everything from a directory

description: Hidden large dependencies silently inflate your bundle
do: Run bundle analyzer regularly and set size budgets in CI
dont: Don't add dependencies without checking their bundle impact with bundlephobia

description: Unoptimized images are the largest source of page weight
do: Use next/image or a CDN with automatic format conversion and resizing
dont: Don't serve raw PNGs or uncompressed JPEGs — convert to WebP or AVIF

description: Web fonts cause layout shift and block rendering if loaded incorrectly
do: Use font-display swap with preloaded font files and subset unused glyphs
dont: Don't load entire font families — subset to only the weights and characters needed

description: Loading all CSS upfront delays first contentful paint
do: Extract critical CSS inline and lazy-load the rest with media or async loading
dont: Don't bundle all styles into a single CSS file for large applications

description: Third-party scripts block rendering and compete for main thread time
do: Load third-party scripts with async or defer and delay non-essential ones
dont: Don't place analytics or tag manager scripts in the document head without async

description: Server Components reduce client bundle by keeping rendering on the server
do: Default to Server Components and add use client only when interactivity is needed
dont: Don't add use client to components that only render static or fetched data

description: Streaming SSR sends HTML progressively reducing time to first byte
do: Use renderToPipeableStream with Suspense for progressive page delivery
dont: Don't block the entire page response waiting for slow data sources

description: ISR serves cached pages and regenerates in the background for freshness
do: Use revalidate with appropriate intervals based on content change frequency
dont: Don't use SSR for pages that change infrequently — ISR is more efficient

description: API routes without caching re-execute on every request increasing TTFB
do: Add Cache-Control headers and use edge runtime for latency-sensitive routes
dont: Don't run heavy computation in API routes without caching or background jobs

description: Middleware runs on every request — heavy logic degrades all page loads
do: Keep middleware lightweight — use it for redirects auth checks and headers only
dont: Don't fetch data or run business logic in middleware — it runs on every request

description: Proper caching headers eliminate redundant server work and speed repeat visits
do: Set Cache-Control with stale-while-revalidate for dynamic and immutable for hashed assets
dont: Don't serve dynamic content without any caching headers — every request hits origin

description: Edge runtime reduces latency by running code closer to the user
do: Use edge runtime for auth middleware redirects and personalization logic
dont: Don't use edge runtime for heavy computation or Node.js-specific APIs

description: Server Actions simplify mutations by eliminating manual API endpoint creation
do: Use server actions for form submissions and data mutations with useActionState
dont: Don't use server actions for read operations — they are designed for mutations

description: Incorrect memoization adds complexity without performance benefit
do: Use useMemo for expensive computations and useCallback for stable handler references
dont: Don't memoize primitive values or cheap calculations — it adds overhead not savings

description: React.memo prevents rerenders when props haven't changed via shallow comparison
do: Wrap components that receive the same props frequently but rerender due to parent updates
dont: Don't use React.memo on components that almost always receive different props

description: Global state for local concerns causes unnecessary rerenders across the tree
do: Keep state as close as possible to where it's used — lift only when truly shared
dont: Don't put form input state or UI toggles in global state management

description: A single large context causes all consumers to rerender on any value change
do: Split context by update frequency — separate static config from frequently changing state
dont: Don't put rapidly changing values like mouse position in the same context as theme config

description: Rendering thousands of DOM nodes causes jank and high memory usage
do: Use TanStack Virtual or react-window for lists with more than 100 items
dont: Don't render all list items into the DOM — virtualize any list that could grow large

description: Runtime CSS-in-JS inserts styles on every render adding main thread work
do: Use zero-runtime CSS-in-JS like vanilla-extract or CSS modules for performance
dont: Don't use runtime styled-components in performance-critical render paths

description: Creating new function references on every render causes child rerenders
do: Define handlers outside render or wrap with useCallback for stable references
dont: Don't define inline arrow functions in JSX for components wrapped in React.memo

description: Using state for values that don't affect rendering causes unnecessary rerenders
do: Use useRef for mutable values that don't need to trigger rerenders
dont: Don't use useState for tracking intervals timers or previous values — use useRef

description: Wasted rerenders accumulate and cause visible jank especially on lower-end devices
do: Use React DevTools Profiler to identify components that rerender without visible change
dont: Don't ignore components that rerender on every parent update — investigate and fix

description: React 18 automatically batches state updates but some patterns break batching
do: Rely on automatic batching — group related state updates in event handlers
dont: Don't use flushSync unless you specifically need synchronous DOM updates

description: Using useEffect to sync state from props creates extra render cycles and bugs
do: Compute derived values during render or use useMemo for expensive derivations
dont: Don't useEffect to set state based on other state or props — compute it inline

description: Incorrect key props cause unnecessary unmount-remount cycles or stale state
do: Use stable unique identifiers as keys — database IDs or content-derived keys
dont: Don't use array index as key for lists that are filtered sorted or modified

description: Passing children as JSX prevents parent rerenders from affecting child subtree
do: Use children prop for content that shouldn't rerender when parent state changes
dont: Don't inline complex JSX that could be passed as children to a wrapper component

description: Compound components share state implicitly avoiding prop drilling rerenders
do: Use compound component pattern with context for tightly coupled UI groups
dont: Don't pass callbacks through 5 levels of props — use compound pattern or context

description: Render prop functions create new closures on every render breaking memoization
do: Memoize render prop callbacks or convert to compound component pattern
dont: Don't pass inline render functions to memoized children — it defeats React.memo

description: Concurrent features let React prioritize urgent updates over expensive ones
do: Use useTransition for expensive state updates and useDeferredValue for derived values
dont: Don't wrap every state update in startTransition — only use for non-urgent updates

description: Performance regressions ship to production without automated performance testing
do: Add performance benchmarks for critical render paths and track in CI
dont: Don't rely only on manual testing — automate performance regression detection

description: Lighthouse CI catches performance regressions before they reach production
do: Run Lighthouse CI on pull requests with score thresholds and size budgets
dont: Don't skip Lighthouse checks — a single large dependency can tank your score

description: Bundle size creep happens gradually and is hard to notice without monitoring
do: Set bundle size budgets in CI and fail builds that exceed limits
dont: Don't merge PRs that increase bundle size without justification and review

description: Lab metrics alone miss real-user performance issues across devices and networks
do: Track Core Web Vitals with real user monitoring using web-vitals library
dont: Don't rely only on lab data — field metrics reveal actual user experience issues
